title: Martial Arts
wikitext:
[[File:Martial-arts-21.jpg|center]]

==Summary==
'''Martial Arts''' is defined as '''different techniques of attacking and self-defense, with the purpose of physical and/or spiritual self-improvement.''' Some fictional characters (especially if they have attained mastery), often pass regular limits and become akin to superhuman. For our purposes, martial arts is given to those and only those who possess notable skill in unarmed combat. Characters skilled only in the usage of weapons receive [[Weapon Mastery]] instead. It is encouraged to specify which martial art a character specializes in, if it is known.

==Possible Uses==
*Obtaining [[Superhuman Physical Characteristics|superhuman physical characteristics]] (on a temporary or even permanent basis, or to imitate such characteristics via [[Chi Manipulation|ki manipulation]])
*Repel attacks of an enemy back at them, or use their energy to strengthen oneself.
*Neutralize the opponent's attack or partially reduce its power.
*Control your body in a variety of ways.
*Attack vulnerable points of the enemy (hurting, paralyzing or even killing by a touch); can be labelled as [[Pressure Points|pressure points]], with strikes aimed at bones, joints and internal organs.
*Attack with [[Chi Manipulation|internal energy]]; can lead to increased strike impact because of ki, and also generating powerful [[Energy Manipulation|energy attacks]].

==Users==
*[[Kenshiro (Hokuto no Ken)|Kenshiro]] ([[Fist of the North Star]])
*[[Ryu (Street Fighter)|Ryu]] ([[Street Fighter]])
*[[Nightwing (Post-Crisis)|Nightwing]] ([[DC Comics]])
*[[Kazuma Kiryu]] ([[Like a Dragon|Yakuza]])
*[[John Wick]] ([[John Wick (Verse)|John Wick]])
